name: ThingsBoard IoT Platform REST API
what_it_does: Open-source IoT platform with REST API — enabling enterprises and developers to manage IoT devices at scale with MQTT, CoAP, HTTP, and LwM2M device protocols, time-series data storage, rule engine for automated event processing, customizable dashboards, multi-tenancy, and both community open-source and enterprise cloud/SaaS deployment options.
use_cases: Managing IoT device registration and provisioning via ThingsBoard REST API for fleet management
Querying time-series telemetry data via ThingsBoard API for IoT analytics and reporting
Configuring ThingsBoard rule chains via API for automated IoT event processing
Managing ThingsBoard tenants and customers via API for multi-tenant IoT SaaS applications
Sending RPC commands to IoT devices via ThingsBoard API for remote device control
not_for: Simple maker/hobbyist projects (ThingsBoard is enterprise-grade — use Blynk for simple projects)
Teams without Java/DevOps expertise (self-hosted requires Java + PostgreSQL + Cassandra)
Real-time sub-100ms device control (ThingsBoard adds latency via rule engine)
best_when: An enterprise or industrial team needs a scalable, self-hosted or cloud IoT platform supporting multiple protocols (MQTT, CoAP, HTTP, LwM2M) with rule-based event processing, multi-tenancy, and time-series analytics.
avoid_when: You're building a simple hobby project, need sub-100ms control loops, or lack DevOps resources to operate self-hosted Java infrastructure.

auth.notes: JWT token auth via POST /api/auth/login with username/password. Device API uses device access token (per-device key). Management API uses JWT. OAuth2/SAML support for enterprise SSO. Multi-tenant RBAC with tenant admin, customer user, and sub-customer roles. Webhook via Rule Engine (HTTP node). Separate device credentials from user credentials. ThingsBoard Cloud (SaaS) or self-hosted.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community Edition: open source, self-hosted, unlimited devices (Apache 2.0)
pricing.paid_tiers: ThingsBoard Cloud Maker $10/month; Prototype $50/month; Startup $200/month; Enterprise custom; PE (Professional) on-premises custom
pricing.notes: Ukrainian company (Kyiv). Founded 2016 by Andrew Shvaika. Apache 2.0 open source (Community Edition). Professional Edition (PE) is paid on-premises. ThingsBoard Cloud (SaaS) is the managed option. 15,000+ GitHub stars. Written in Java. PostgreSQL + Cassandra/TimescaleDB for storage. MQTT, CoAP, HTTP, LwM2M, SNMP device protocols. LoRaWAN integration. Widgets library (200+). Rule Engine (event-driven logic). Multi-tenancy. Edge computing (ThingsBoard Edge). Used by industrial IoT, smart energy, smart city projects globally. Ukrainian origin — active community despite war impact.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: unlimited; Cloud: by plan tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with JWT + device tokens. Webhook via Rule Engine. Java SDK, Python SDK. Open source — full API source inspectable. Good documentation. Multi-tenant RBAC. Community Edition self-hosted is free. JWT token must be refreshed (auth/token endpoint). Ukrainian company with strong developer community.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Device reads idempotent by device ID. Telemetry reads idempotent by time range. Device creation idempotent by name+tenant. Rule chain reads idempotent. RPC commands not idempotent — each call sends command.
known_agent_gotchas: JWT token expires — implement token refresh for long-running agents
Device credentials separate from user credentials — two auth systems to manage
Self-hosted requires Java + PostgreSQL + Cassandra — significant infrastructure
Rule Engine for webhooks — configure HTTP node in Rule Chain, not API-based webhooks
Community vs Professional Edition have different features — check edition differences

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. JWT token auth with expiry. Multi-tenant RBAC with tenant/customer isolation. Device credentials separate from management credentials. OAuth2/SAML SSO for enterprise. Open source — auditable Java code. Self-hosted data residency control. Ukrainian origin — evaluate supply chain for sensitive deployments. X.509 certificate auth for devices supported.
